name: Kuwait

visa_types.name: Tourist Visit Visa (Sticker/Embassy Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism and leisure visits to Kuwait. Applicant must have a Kuwaiti resident or citizen sponsor.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 10 KWD per month (approximately USD 33/month). Application submitted in person to the General Department of Immigration in the relevant governorate. Required documents: visit application form, passport copy (minimum 6-month validity), proof of kinship, recent salary certificate or work permit of sponsor, sponsor's Civil ID copy, hotel authorization if applicable. Visa on arrival available at Kuwait International Airport for nationals of 53 eligible countries (see visa_on_arrival policy). Extension possible via separate Temporary Residence extension service.

visa_types.name: Tourist E-Visa
visa_types.purpose: Electronic pre-arrival visa for tourism and leisure visits to Kuwait, available online for nationals of 53 eligible countries and GCC residents.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 3 KWD (approximately USD 10). Available to nationals of 53 countries and GCC residents (excluding Afghan, Bangladeshi, Iranian, Iraqi, Pakistani, Yemeni nationals). Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. Processing 1–3 business days. Land and sea arrivals must obtain e-visa or embassy visa in advance; air arrivals from eligible countries may also use visa on arrival. Portal relaunched January 2025, enhanced June 2026. For multiple-entry options, check portal at time of application.

visa_types.name: Family Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows relatives of Kuwait citizens or residents to visit their family members in Kuwait for a temporary period.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 10 KWD per month. Application submitted to General Department of Immigration. Required documents: designated visit application form, visitor's passport copy (minimum 6-month validity), proof of kinship (embassy oath certificates not accepted for some nationalities), recent salary certificate for government-employed foreign sponsors, sponsor's Civil ID copy. Embassy oath certificates not accepted as proof of kinship; must use official government-issued kinship documents. Both Kuwaiti citizens and non-Kuwaiti residents can sponsor family visitors.

visa_types.name: Family Visit E-Visa
visa_types.purpose: Electronic pre-arrival visa for family visits to Kuwait, for nationals of 53 eligible countries.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 3 KWD (approximately USD 10). Maximum stay 30 days (shorter than tourist e-visa at 90 days). One of four e-visa categories offered via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw: tourist (90 days), family visit (30 days), business (30 days), official. Available to nationals of the same 53 countries eligible for tourist e-visa.

visa_types.name: Medical Treatment Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign nationals seeking medical treatment at Kuwait hospitals or healthcare facilities.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 10 KWD per month. Requires a letter issued by the Kuwait Ministry of Health confirming the visitor has been accepted for treatment at a Kuwait hospital. Required documents: designated visit application form, passport copy (minimum 6-month validity), Ministry of Health acceptance letter, sponsor's Civil ID copy. Application submitted to General Department of Immigration.

visa_types.name: Business Visit E-Visa (Commercial Visit)
visa_types.purpose: Electronic pre-arrival visa for foreign nationals visiting Kuwait for business purposes in the private sector.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 3 KWD (approximately USD 10). Maximum stay 30 days. One of four e-visa categories on kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. Available to nationals of 53 eligible countries. SUSPENSION NOTE: Suspended for all nationalities on 10 June 2026; RESUMED for all nationalities on 19 June 2026 per Fragomen alert. Tourist e-visa category remained operational throughout the suspension. NOTE: A separate Fragomen advisory (September 3, 2025) reports resumption of the sponsored Commercial Visit Visa for Pakistani nationals - this is a single-entry visa issued only at the request of a company registered in Kuwait (employer/sponsor-conditional), NOT a visa-on-arrival nor a general ordinary-passport e-visa grant. Pakistani nationals remain excluded from the standard 53-country e-visa/visa-on-arrival facility. Fragomen is an immigration-advisory firm, not an official Kuwaiti government source.

visa_types.name: Entrance Visa to Work in the Government Sector
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign nationals who have secured employment with the Kuwait government or civil service.
visa_types.notes: For foreign nationals hired by Kuwaiti government entities. Application submitted to General Department of Immigration. Specific fee amounts and visa duration were not published on the official e.gov.kw page at time of research. The e.gov.kw page URL was not accessible directly (HTTP 404) but linked from official portal navigation.

visa_types.name: Work Visa for Temporary Government Contract
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign workers engaged under temporary government contracts administered through the Public Authority for Manpower.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 10 KWD (fixed). Required documents include: letter from the Government Contracts and Projects Department of the Public Authority for Manpower, financial guarantee statement showing worker count and guarantee amount, application form, passport copy (minimum 6-month validity), legal entity signature authorization, valid representative ID. Application submitted in person to General Department of Immigration.

visa_types.name: Business Visit Visa (Private Sector Work Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign nationals seeking employment or business activities in Kuwait's private sector.
visa_types.notes: Listed under 'Working in Kuwait' and 'Moving to Kuwait' sections on the Kuwait Government Online portal (e.gov.kw). Separate from the e-visa business category. For private sector employment. Specific fee amounts and duration were not published on an accessible official page at time of research.

visa_types.name: Entrance Visa to Join Provider (Family Reunification/Dependent Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Dependent entry visa allowing family members to join employees working in Kuwait's government or private sectors.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 10 KWD fixed plus variable fees. Sponsor minimum salary: KWD 450 per month (unless one of 15 exempt professions). Required documents: entry visa application form, sponsored person's passport copy (minimum 2-year validity), sponsor's Civil ID, proof of kinship (passport for Arab nationals; embassy certificate for non-Arab nationals), work permit (private sector) or salary certificate (government sector). For Article 19 holders: license or company contract copy required. Article 24 holders: reviewed at Director General's discretion. Application submitted to General Department of Immigration.

visa_types.name: Government/State Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Official entry visa for foreign government officials and delegations visiting Kuwait on state business.
visa_types.notes: Listed under 'Working in Kuwait' section on e.gov.kw portal. For official government delegations and state visitors. Specific fees and duration not published on an accessible official page at time of research (HTTP 404 on direct page access). Processed through General Department of Immigration.

visa_types.name: Official E-Visa
visa_types.purpose: Electronic pre-arrival visa for foreign nationals visiting Kuwait on official business for government delegations.
visa_types.notes: One of four e-visa categories offered via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw: tourist (90 days), family visit (30 days), business (30 days), official. Specific eligibility criteria and maximum stay duration for official category not specified in accessible official sources. Fee estimated at 3 KWD (same as other e-visa categories).

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence / Visit Extension
visa_types.purpose: Extension of an existing temporary visit or residence permit for foreign nationals already in Kuwait.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 1 KWD fixed plus 1 KWD per month variable (approximately USD 3 fixed + USD 3/month). For individuals with existing temporary residence status seeking to prolong their stay. Required documents: original passport and copy, entry visa documentation, two passport photos, sponsor's Civil ID copy, health insurance certificate, work permit from Ministry of Social Affairs and Labor, signed authorization forms. Application submitted to General Department of Immigration. Residence Application Form must be signed by the sponsor.

visa_types.name: First Time Residence Permit for Civil Sector Employees
visa_types.purpose: Initial residence permit for foreign nationals employed in Kuwait's civil (government) sector for the first time.
visa_types.notes: Listed under 'Immigration and Travel' section on e.gov.kw portal. Specific fees, processing times, and duration not available from accessible official pages at time of research. Part of Kuwait's kafala (employer-sponsor) residency system. Ministry of Interior General Department of Residency Affairs handles applications.

visa_free.nationality: GCC citizens (Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates)
visa_free.notes: GCC citizens may enter Kuwait visa-free using a valid GCC national ID card. GCC freedom-of-movement agreement. Exact maximum stay days not specified on official government page; one source (gulftourz.com) states 'unlimited stay' for GCC citizens; this is not confirmed verbatim from an official Kuwaiti government page.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Tourist visa on arrival (up to 90 days) for air arrivals; alternatively may obtain e-visa before arrival via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. Land/sea arrivals must obtain visa in advance.
Tourist visa on arrival (up to 90 days) for air arrivals or e-visa before arrival.

e_visa.nationality: Citizens of 53 countries (same list as visa_on_arrival above)
e_visa.notes: Citizens of the 53 nationalities listed under visa_on_arrival may alternatively apply for an e-visa before arrival via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. Four categories: tourist (90 days), family visit (30 days), business (30 days), official. E-visa fee: KWD 3. Processing time 1–3 business days. Land/sea arrivals must obtain e-visa or embassy visa in advance. GCC residents (excluding nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, Yemen) may also apply via the e-visa portal. Note: as of June 2026 the commercial visit visa (business category) has been temporarily suspended for all nationalities until further notice per Fragomen alert dated 10 June 2026; tourist e-visa remains operational. Portal upgraded and relaunched January 2025; further enhancements June 2026.

visa_policy.notes: The official Kuwait Ministry of Interior e-visa portal (kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw) serves as the primary authority. The portal is JavaScript-heavy and its full nationality list could not be directly scraped; the 53-country list was corroborated across multiple independent sources referencing the official portal and the US State Department confirming US citizen eligibility. Tourist visa on arrival and tourist e-visa both carry 90-day maximum stay. Family visit and business e-visas are 30 days. GCC citizens (Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) enter visa-free with national ID. All GCC residents (regardless of nationality, with noted exclusions) may obtain tourist visa on arrival since August 10, 2025 (profession restriction removed). Citizens of countries not on the 53-country list and not GCC residents must obtain a visa from a Kuwaiti embassy or consulate before travel. Commercial visit visa (business e-visa category) was briefly suspended 10–19 June 2026 but has since resumed for all nationalities.

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid residence permit (iqama) issued by Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, or Oman with at least 6 months remaining validity at time of travel. Passport must also be valid for at least 6 months on arrival. Confirmed return or onward ticket required. Visa issued at all entry points (air, land, sea). Tourism only - no work permitted. Nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, and Yemen are excluded from this facility and must apply at a Kuwaiti embassy or consulate. Policy effective 10 August 2025; replaced previous profession-based restriction that limited eligibility to doctors, engineers, lawyers, teachers, journalists, and business managers.

Nationals of most countries holding a valid US visa or US residence permit may apply for a simplified Kuwait e-Visa. The supporting document must be valid for at least 6 months at the time of application. E-Visa may be issued as single or multiple-entry; each stay maximum 30 days; e-Visa itself valid up to 1 year depending on applicant eligibility and visa type. Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. NOTE: This rule is referenced in multiple travel-industry sources but could not be independently confirmed from the official Kuwait portal (JavaScript-rendered); source_official is therefore false.

Nationals of most countries holding a valid US residence permit may apply for a simplified Kuwait e-Visa. The supporting document must be valid for at least 6 months at the time of application. Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. NOTE: This rule is referenced in multiple travel-industry sources but could not be independently confirmed from the official Kuwait portal (JavaScript-rendered); source_official is therefore false.

Nationals of most countries holding a valid Schengen Area visa may apply for a simplified Kuwait e-Visa. The supporting document must be valid for at least 6 months at the time of application. Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. NOTE: This rule is referenced in multiple travel-industry sources but could not be independently confirmed from the official Kuwait portal (JavaScript-rendered); source_official is therefore false.

Nationals of most countries holding a valid Schengen Area residence permit may apply for a simplified Kuwait e-Visa. The supporting document must be valid for at least 6 months at the time of application. Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. NOTE: This rule is referenced in multiple travel-industry sources but could not be independently confirmed from the official Kuwait portal (JavaScript-rendered); source_official is therefore false.

Nationals of most countries holding a valid United Kingdom visa may apply for a simplified Kuwait e-Visa. The supporting document must be valid for at least 6 months at the time of application. Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. NOTE: This rule is referenced in multiple travel-industry sources but could not be independently confirmed from the official Kuwait portal (JavaScript-rendered); source_official is therefore false.

Nationals of most countries holding a valid United Kingdom residence permit may apply for a simplified Kuwait e-Visa. The supporting document must be valid for at least 6 months at the time of application. Apply via kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw. NOTE: This rule is referenced in multiple travel-industry sources but could not be independently confirmed from the official Kuwait portal (JavaScript-rendered); source_official is therefore false.

Holders of Indian diplomatic, official, and special passports do not require a visa for Kuwait for up to 60 days. Bilateral visa exemption agreement signed 04 April 2018, entered into force 08 March 2019. Ordinary Indian passport holders require a visa.

Holders of Japanese diplomatic and official passports do not require a visa for Kuwait. Reciprocal visa exemption arrangement effective 12 May 2025. Ordinary Japanese passport holders are eligible for visa on arrival (90 days). Agreement confirmed by Ambassador Mukai Kenichiro; applies to both Kuwaiti diplomatic/special passports in Japan (90 days) and Japanese diplomatic/official passports in Kuwait (reciprocal).

cbi.notes: Kuwait has no citizenship-by-investment program. Kuwaiti nationality law (Emiri Decree No. 15 of 1959, as amended) does not provide for acquisition of citizenship through financial investment. Naturalization requires 20 years of continuous legal residency for non-Arab nationals and 15 years for Arab nationals, plus Arabic language proficiency and other requirements. A new citizenship law effective 13 April 2026 tightened requirements. Dual citizenship is not permitted; naturalized citizens must renounce prior nationality within 3 months. Source: nat.moi.gov.kw (official Ministry of Interior nationality department).

programs.name: Long-Term Investor Residency – Real Estate Route
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who purchase property in Kuwait may qualify for a 10-year renewable residency permit under Article 7 of the Executive Regulations of the Law on the Residence of Foreigners. Effective date: 23 December 2025. No fixed minimum investment amount specified; applications reviewed case-by-case. Does not lead to permanent residency or citizenship. Source: KPMG Flash Alert 2025-272 citing Kuwait News Agency (official government media) and Ministry of Interior.

programs.name: Long-Term Investor Residency – Business Route
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who establish or invest in a qualifying business in Kuwait may obtain a 15-year renewable residency permit under Article 7 of the Executive Regulations of the Law on the Residence of Foreigners. Must comply with Kuwait Direct Investment Promotion Authority (KDIPA) Law No. 116 of 2013. Criteria include technology transfer, economic diversification support, job creation for Kuwaiti nationals. Projects on the Cabinet's Negative List (oil/gas, media, certain manufacturing) are excluded. No fixed minimum investment amount; case-by-case review. Effective date: 23 December 2025. Does not lead to permanent residency or citizenship. Fee: KD 10 per month per Ministerial Decision No. 2249/2025.

programs.name: Freelance Residency Permit
programs.eligibility: Skilled foreign professionals, consultants, and small-scale entrepreneurs seeking to work in Kuwait without a traditional kafala employer sponsor. Specific profession lists and income thresholds pending final regulations as of June 2026.
programs.processing_time: Not yet officially specified; program announced February 2026 with rollout expected within approximately 2 months of announcement; official regulations had not been published as of June 2026.
programs.notes: Announced by Sheikh Fahad Al-Yousef Al-Sabah, First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, on 17 February 2026. Annual fee of KD 750–1,000. Marks a shift away from the kafala (employer-sponsor) residency model. Full eligibility criteria, documentation requirements, and family sponsorship rules had not been published as of June 2026. Nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, and Yemen were flagged as excluded from e-visa-based routes generally; applicability to this permit pending confirmation.

agreements: GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) – freedom of movement and residence for GCC citizens

validation_notes: VALIDATION RUN 2026-06-29: Re-fetched US State Dept (travel.state.gov - confirmed VoA 90 days, fee 10 KWD for US citizens), India MEA (mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport - confirmed diplomatic/official/special 60 days), kuwaitimmigration.org 53-country list (confirmed Bulgaria and all 53 countries unchanged), Fragomen commercial visit visa page (fragomen.com/insights/kuwait-commercial-visit-visa-issuance-temporarily-suspended.html). Official sources reached: 2 (US State Dept, India MEA). KEY CORRECTION: Commercial visit visa (business e-visa category) previously noted as 'temporarily suspended as of June 2026' — CORRECTED: suspended 10 June 2026, RESUMED 19 June 2026 for all nationalities per Fragomen. Updated business visa notes and visa_policy.notes accordingly. 53-country VoA/e-visa list confirmed unchanged. Bulgarian citizens confirmed eligible (in 53-country list). GCC resident VoA confirmed unchanged. No new nationalities added or removed from visa_free, visa_on_arrival, e_visa, or eta lists.

VALIDATION RUN 2026-06-27: Re-fetched US State Dept (travel.state.gov), India MEA (mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport), Kuwait Airport official page (kuwaitairport.gov.kw), Kuwait e-visa portal (kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw - JavaScript-rendered, no extractable content), MOFA pages (HTTP 403 Forbidden). Official sources reached: 3 (US State Dept, India MEA, Kuwait Airport). Key finding: GCC resident VoA confirmed available at ALL entry points (air, land, sea) as of 10 August 2025 - no change needed as existing conditional_access entry already reflects this. Business e-visa suspension confirmed still in effect for most nationalities; ADDED: Pakistani nationals exception - commercial visit visa resumed September 3, 2025 per Fragomen (NOT April 2026 as previously misrecorded). India diplomatic/official/special passport exemption re-confirmed (60 days, signed 04.04.2018, in force 08.03.2019). 53-country VoA/e-visa list unchanged. No new nationalities added or removed from any list.

CORRECTED: Prior record stated visa_on_arrival notes as '3-month validity' but did not have max_stay_days=90 clearly aligned; confirmed via US State Department (travel.state.gov) that US citizens receive 'up to three months (90 days)'; max_stay_days=90 is correct and retained.
CORRECTED: Prior e-visa entry notes stated 'July 2025 launch'; clarified to note the portal was suspended December 2024, relaunched January 16, 2025, with further enhancements June 2026.

CORRECTED: Added note to e_visa entry that the commercial visit (business) e-visa category has been temporarily suspended as of June 2026 (Fragomen alert, 10 June 2026); tourist e-visa remains active.

CORRECTED: conditional_access entries for US/Schengen/UK credential-based e-visa rules changed from source_official=true to source_official=false; these rules are widely cited in travel industry sources but could not be confirmed directly from the official Kuwait portal (which is JavaScript-rendered and returned no extractable content) or from any MOFA page (which returned HTTP 403 on all subpages).

CORRECTED: conditional_access GCC resident VoA entry changed source_official from true to false; while the policy is confirmed, the exact conditions (profession restriction lift, 90-day stay) were sourced from Wego blog and Middle East Briefing, not directly from the official MOI portal.

CORRECTED: Added prior profession-based restriction detail to GCC resident VoA conditions (approved professions: doctors, engineers, lawyers, teachers, journalists, business managers) as confirmed by multiple sources.

RETAINED: 53-country list for visa_on_arrival is consistent across all cross-referenced sources (Fragomen, GPA, Travelobiz, VisasNews, kuwaitimmigration.org). One source (visamundi.co) listed Qatar and UAE additionally but these are GCC members who enter visa-free; those additions were not adopted.
RETAINED: Japan diplomatic/official passport exemption kept as source_official=false; no official MOFA/MoI page confirming this was accessible.
RETAINED: India diplomatic/official/special passport exemption source_official=true via India MEA (mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport) which is an official government domain.
RETAINED: RBI entry note updated to mention KD 10/month fee per Ministerial Decision No. 2249/2025 (effective 23 December 2025) per Gulf News report.
RETAINED: Freelance Residency Permit status note updated to confirm regulations had still not been officially published as of June 2026.

ADDED: visa_types array populated from official e.gov.kw Kuwait Government Online portal pages (June 2026). Fee of 10 KWD/month confirmed for tourist visit, family visit, medical treatment, and dependent entry visas. Temporary Government Contract Work Visa fee confirmed as 10 KWD fixed. Visit extension fee confirmed as 1 KWD fixed + 1 KWD/month variable. E-visa fee 3 KWD confirmed from multiple sources referencing official portal.
gaps: The official Kuwait e-visa portal (kuwaitvisa.moi.gov.kw and evisa.moi.gov.kw) is heavily JavaScript-rendered and returned no extractable nationality lists on re-fetch (June 2026); the 53-country list was corroborated from multiple independent sources referencing the official portal, but was not confirmed verbatim from the official page itself.

Exact maximum stay duration for GCC citizens (visa-free category) not confirmed on any official government page; gulftourz.com states 'unlimited stay' but this is not from an official Kuwait government domain.
Minimum investment amounts for both investor residency routes (real estate and business) are not published officially; only 'case-by-case review' language confirmed.
The freelance residency permit (announced February 2026) had not published final regulations as of June 2026; official URL and full eligibility criteria unavailable.
The mofa.gov.kw domain returned HTTP 403 for all subpages attempted on re-fetch, limiting direct Ministry of Foreign Affairs data extraction.
The full official text of the GCC free movement agreement as applied to Kuwait was not retrievable from a primary Kuwaiti government source.
No ETA (Electronic Travel Authorization) scheme found for Kuwait - confirmed absent.

The US/Schengen/UK visa credential-based conditional access rules (allowing holders of those credentials to obtain Kuwait e-visa) are widely referenced in travel industry sources but cannot be confirmed directly from the official portal due to JavaScript rendering. These are retained with source_official=false.

The Japan-Kuwait diplomatic/official passport visa exemption (effective 12 May 2025) was sourced from a media report (Times Kuwait) citing an official diplomatic exchange, not directly from mofa.gov.kw or MOFA Japan; source_official=false.

Wikipedia lists 35+ bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport visa exemptions for Kuwait beyond India and Japan; these remain unrecorded as the Kuwait MOFA website returns HTTP 403 and Wikipedia is not an official source.
Commercial visit visa (business e-visa category) was suspended 10 June 2026 and resumed 19 June 2026 per Fragomen; no longer a gap as of 2026-06-29.
Freelance Residency Permit: applicability to nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Iran, Iraq, Pakistan, Yemen not officially confirmed.
visa_types entries for Government Sector Work Visa and State Visit Visa could not be fetched directly (HTTP 404); details sourced from navigation links on e.gov.kw homepage.

Maximum stay days and validity for most in-country visa types (tourist visit, family visit, medical, work visas) not published on accessible official pages; fee of 10 KWD/month implies month-based duration.

visa_free.name: United Arab Emirates (GCC – Civil ID only)

outbound_official.notes: Source is the official Kuwait Government Online portal (e.gov.kw) page titled 'List of countries that grant Kuwaiti citizens entry visas upon arrival or require a pre-issued visa from the embassy'. GCC countries (Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE) are accessible with Civil ID only (no passport required). Some destinations (e.g. Hong Kong, Indonesia, Laos, Mongolia, Nepal) appear in both visa_free and visa_on_arrival categories as listed on the source page. No date stamp was present on the source page at time of retrieval (2026-06-14).
